category.evidence: We are Little Friends Learning Loft, a preschool inside the Newburgh JCC. Play-based, warm, small. Ages 2 to pre-K.

history.value: Play-based preschool housed inside the Newburgh JCC, serving ages 2 through pre-K for approximately 14 years.

value.status: claimed, current state uncertain to owner

gbp.evidence: Google Business Profile, I claimed it once, I think.

hours.value: Mon–Fri 8:00am–5:30pm; early drop at 7:45am; follows JCC calendar plus Jewish holidays
hours.evidence: 8 to 5:30, early drop at 7:45. We follow the JCC calendar mostly, plus Jewish holidays, which trips up families who do not realize.

value.name: JCC (Jewish Community Center)
value.role: Parent organization — exec director and board sit above the director

value.name: JCC Executive Director
value.role: Above Rebecca; board-level decisions route through her

keyTeam.evidence: Me obviously. My three leads, Dana, Carla, Tova, they are the heart of it. Assistants in each room. An aftercare person, Yael. Linda the bookkeeper, part-time, JCC. The JCC has an exec director above me, anything big goes through her and the board.

value.trigger: billing dispute or purely billing question

rules.evidence: That should route to me, or Linda if it is purely billing.

value: Classroom assistants — not their job and not fair to them

secondary.evidence: Fall open house, the holiday programs through the J, a spring fundraiser which is our big one, parent-ed nights, monthly story time that is open to the community.

value: Infant care — licensed two-and-up only; high inbound demand but always declined

seasonal.evidence: Summer camp June through August, that is a different animal, mixed ages, more enrichment.

value.conditions: Tuition is intentionally NOT published; shared only after a tour

value.askedQuestion: What is tuition / how much does it cost?
value.deflectionAnswer: I'd love to show you the space first — tuition depends on the program and days. Let's get you in for a tour; the number out of context loses families who would have loved us.

deposit.value: One month's tuition, due at enrollment, non-refundable

paymentSchedule.evidence: Monthly, full-time Pre-K is around eleven-eighty

visibilityPolicy.value: Do not lead with price; invite to tour first; explain tuition depends on program and days selected

value.typical: Enrollment targets the September school-year start; tours run year-round (busiest winter–spring, slow in summer)
value.minimum: [NEEDS CONFIRMATION] not stated in the corpus

engagementTypes.evidence: less for JCC members, less for part-time days

primaryCustomer.evidence: A parent emails asking about a spot for their two-year-old.

discoveryChannels.evidence: Word of mouth, mostly. Some from the JCC. A few say they Googled but I am not sure I believe it.

value: touring the facility in person
meeting the director and teachers
seeing the environment before learning price
decisionCriteria.evidence: the number scares people before they have seen the place and met us

value: other local preschools
optionsConsidered.evidence: by then they toured somewhere else.

priceSensitivity.evidence: Because the number out of context loses people who would have loved us.

keyDifferentiator.evidence: Small, warm, they meet me and the teachers and it is clearly not a factory. And the J community, for some families that matters a lot.

comparison.value: The warm small one vs. Goddard's polished, slick, more expensive positioning
comparison.evidence: We are the warm small one, not the slick one.

recommendedStatement.value: [operator gap-fill, synthetic e2e] Little Friends Learning Loft is the warm, small, JCC-rooted preschool in Newburgh for ages 2–5 — parents meet the director and lead teachers and leave a tour feeling calmer, not sold to. (Recommended positioning statement; operator analysis, confirm with owner.)

value.definition: A frazzled parent should feel calmer after reading us, not sold to
value.example: I hear you, the first couple weeks can be tender, and that is so normal.

toneSpectrum.evidence: I want a frazzled parent to feel calmer after reading me, not sold to.

value: Never make a parent feel like they are talking to a robot about their kid
antiAiRules.evidence: Never make a parent feel like they are talking to a robot about their kid.

value: An unnamed pediatrician newsletter — described as very plain and kind
admiredVoices.evidence: There is a pediatrician newsletter I get, very plain and kind. I like that. I could not name it though.

sampleCommunications.evidence: I would say something like, I hear you, the first couple weeks can be tender, and that is so normal. Tova has shepherded a hundred shy twos and she is wonderful at it. Why do not you come spend twenty minutes in the room with us before the start date. We will figure it out together. Something like that.

value: family sends email or Instagram message
Rebecca replies (typically same evening after teaching day ends)
tour scheduled via Sign-Up Genius after back-and-forth exchange
Rebecca personally conducts 45–60 minute tour
steps.evidence: A parent emails asking about a spot for their two-year-old. I see it Tuesday night because I was teaching all day. I write back, we set up a tour, that is a whole back and forth, Sign-Up Genius, then I do the tour, forty-five minutes, sometimes an hour

responseTime.evidence: I see it Tuesday night because I was teaching all day.

value: Child age 2–5 (NOT infants — licensed 2 and up; infants are the do-not-promise line)
Seeking Twos / Threes / Pre-K, full-time, part-time days, or aftercare
Willing to tour before discussing tuition

value.name: In-person tour
value.location: Little Friends Learning Loft, Newburgh NY
value.conversionRate: [NEEDS CONFIRMATION] not precisely tracked — owner estimates 'more than half' enroll when she actually follows up

definition.value: Family completes enrollment application and pays deposit

dropOff.value: Post-tour follow-up often not completed; families tour competing schools before Rebecca reconnects
dropOff.evidence: I do a great tour and then I disappear for two weeks because I am drowning, and by then they toured somewhere else.

value.scenario: After a tour (the current leak — owner goes quiet ~2 weeks and families enroll elsewhere)
steps: Same-day warm thank-you in the owner’s voice
Day 14: final warm follow-up before marking the family dormant

value.rate: more than half when follow-up occurs

conversionRates.evidence: Most of them, honestly, when I actually follow up. If I had to guess, more than half. The losses are almost always me going quiet, not them saying no.

revenuePerCustomer.value: ≈ $1,180/month full-time Pre-K (≈ $14,160/yr) before the JCC 10% discount; Threes slightly less; Twos varies by days. Roughly $12k–$14k/yr per enrolled child depending on program and discounts.

dataQualityNotes.value: Director does not track conversion metrics; all funnel data lives in email inbox and director memory
dataQualityNotes.evidence: if you ask me our conversion rate I will laugh at you.

storage.value: Teachers post to Slack channel named 'photos'; secondary archive on Instagram
storage.evidence: Teachers shoot on their phones, post to a Slack channel called photos. Hundreds by now. The Instagram archive is another pile.

rights.value: Paper permission form per child's file; working digital spreadsheet months out of date; 6 families have no-photo status this year but identities not readily retrievable without pulling physical files

value: Permission source of truth is paper only; digital spreadsheet stale by months
Cannot identify 6 no-photo families without physically pulling files
No automated gate preventing posting images of restricted children — identified as liability risk
gaps.evidence: Permissions, I have a paper form in each child's file, and a spreadsheet that is months out of date. Six families this year are no-photo. I could not tell you offhand which six without the files.

rights.value: Almost no video — a few shaky phone clips; no formal video usage rights established. Owner wants a short welcome video. Apply the same paper-permission gate used for photos to any future video.

caps.value: [operator gap-fill, synthetic e2e] API/automation spend caps to be set at provisioning; not yet established. Owner flagged unknown/forgotten subscriptions as a worry (not itemized).

recurringTasks.evidence: Inquiries and tour scheduling. The newsletter, weekly, I pull from what teachers post and it takes me two hours easy. Payroll hours to Linda, every two weeks. Ordering supplies, snacks, classroom stuff. The photos. Immunization and training tracking. Brightwheel parent messages all day. Event setup. Chasing the occasional late payment. The board report once a month, which I dread. And a barter thing, we trade space with a music teacher and I track it on paper.

value.cadence: Weekly during the summer build; monthly once stable (aligns with the monthly board report)

value: Child immunization records and exemption forms
Staff training certifications (15-hr OCFS, MAT, CPR/first aid) with rolling expiration dates
Fire and shelter drill logs
Photo permission forms per child
regulatedData.evidence: Every child needs current immunizations on file, with the religious or medical exemptions documented properly. Every staff needs fifteen hours of training every two years across specific topics, plus MAT certification for the ones who give medication, plus CPR and first aid. They expire on rolling dates. We do fire and shelter drills on a schedule and log them.

dataRetention.value: [operator gap-fill, synthetic e2e] Child records (immunizations, paper permission forms in each child’s file) retained per OCFS requirements; permission source-of-truth is paper, working spreadsheet is stale. Formal retention schedule [NEEDS CONFIRMATION] — not stated in the corpus.

value.credentialRotation: Rotate all credentials to JCC ownership at offboarding. Today every account (Square, Instagram, Google) is held by the director on a sticky note — the account-ownership gap flagged in the corpus.

primaryOutcome.value: Be back in a classroom three days a week and trust that the office is not on fire
primaryOutcome.evidence: Be back in a classroom three days a week and trust that the office is not on fire. That is it. That is the whole dream.

urgencyTimeline.value: System must be live before September (re-enrollment and fall tour season)
urgencyTimeline.evidence: it would have to be live for September. Re-enrollment and fall tours are the busy season, I cannot be learning a new thing in October.

budgetConstraints.value: Nonprofit organization; budget is constrained; any expenditure requires JCC board approval

value: Never publish tuition
Never show a child's face without the form
Never make a parent feel like they are talking to a robot about their kid
Keep the teachers' names but not their personal details public

engagementScope.evidence: The inquiry-to-tour flow handled. The newsletter assembling itself. The compliance tracking that reminds itself. The photo pipeline solved with permissions built in. A new site that Google and the AI search engines can actually read, that doubles as your front door for tours and events and donations. And the office knowledge written down so it is not living in your head.
